root_text.an5.171:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.an5.171:2.1: “Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato upāsako sārajjaṁ okkanto hoti.
root_text.an5.171:2.3: Pāṇātipātī hoti, adinnādāyī hoti, kāmesumicchācārī hoti, musāvādī hoti, surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhāyī hoti.
root_text.an5.171:2.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato upāsako sārajjaṁ okkanto hoti.
root_text.an5.171:3.1: Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato upāsako visārado hoti.
root_text.an5.171:3.3: Pāṇātipātā paṭivirato hoti, adinnādānā paṭivirato hoti, kāmesumicchācārā paṭivirato hoti, musāvādā paṭivirato hoti, surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhānā paṭivirato hoti.
root_text.an5.171:3.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato upāsako visārado hotī”ti.

translation_text.an5.171:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.an5.171:1.3: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.an5.171:2.1: “A lay follower with five qualities is overcome by timidity.
translation_text.an5.171:2.3: They kill living creatures, steal, commit sexual misconduct, lie, and consume beer, wine, and liquor intoxicants.
translation_text.an5.171:2.4: A lay follower with these five qualities is overcome by timidity.
translation_text.an5.171:3.1: A lay follower with five qualities is self-assured.
translation_text.an5.171:3.3: They don’t kill living creatures, steal, commit sexual misconduct, lie, or consume beer, wine, and liquor intoxicants.
translation_text.an5.171:3.4: A lay follower with these five qualities is self-assured.”
